observations.recommendation:
a. City Accountant to (i) make the necessary adjustments in the books of account
on the noted discrepancies in the CIB accounts, including the reversion of stale checks
to appropriate accounts, and (ii) coordinate with the City Treasurer to closely monitor
and regularly reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction
of errors;

b. City Treasurer to (i) take efforts that all issued checks in payment of valid
obligations be promptly released to the claimants at the soonest possible time to
prevent checks from becoming stale; and (ii) strictly observe the provisions under
Section 59 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs particularly on stale checks; and

c. The City Accountant, in close coordination with the City Treasurer, exert
efforts in resolving outstanding/bank reconciling items and prepares the necessary
adjustments in the books.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) to instruct the:
a. City Treasurer to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers from the submitted
Assessment Roll by the City Assessor; and thereafter, update the RPTAR/RPTIC and
furnish the same to the City Accountant as basis for the preparation of subsidiary
ledger;
b. City Assessor in coordination with the City Treasurer to consider establishing
a computer-assisted database for the City’s Real Property Tax Administration
(RPTA) system to provide more accurate and reliable records at any point in time; and
c. City Accountant to prepare a subsidiary ledger per individual account based
on certified list of real property taxpayers to support the general ledger accounts and,
regularly reconcile the balance of RPT/SET Receivable accounts with the list of
taxpayers with paid/unpaid real property tax dues maintained by the City Treasurer.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive to:
a. require from the concerned officials and employees/persons liable, in
coordination with the CHRMO Department Head, and City Treasurer, the immediate
settlement of all long-overdue audit disallowances pursuant to COA Resolution No.
2015-031 dated August 20, 2015; and
b. instruct the City Accountant to reconcile the balances of the subject
disallowances with pertinent records and thereafter, make the necessary adjustment
in the books.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the LCE require the:
a. City Budget Officer, City Accountant and City Treasurer to closely coordinate
in monitoring the cash position of the LGU to ensure that all disbursements are
properly managed and controlled so that maturing obligations are paid accordingly;
and
b. City Accountant to (i) prepare and submit Subsidiary Ledgers and detailed AP
schedules to facilitate reconciliation and monitoring of the movement of each
creditor’s account and thereby come up with the aging of Accounts Payable; and (ii)
conduct a thorough review and reconciliation of accounts and effect the correction of
errors noted in the recording and misclassification of accounts.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the LCE require the:

a. Inventory and Disposal Committee to: (i) continue with the City’s inventory-taking of
all properties and henceforth, submit the complete RPCPPE including the Report on
Physical Count of Local Road Network System with detailed information in the
prescribed format; and (ii) in coordination with the CGSO Head, to prepare and
submit the IIRUP as basis in recording the de recognition from the books of the
unserviceable properties including motor vehicles, and immediately facilitate the
disposal thereof to prevent further deterioration pursuant to Section 79 of PD No.
1445;

b. CGSO Head and City Accountant in coordination with the City Engineer, Treasurer
and Assessor to maintain complete and updated records of Property Cards (PC)
including Local Road Network Property Cards for all roads and its component under
the City’s jurisdiction; and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC), for
all PPE accounts per asset category including Real Property Ledger Cards for Land,
respectively; and

c. City Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts on the
acquired lots and motor vehicles totaling ₱17,280,000.00 and ₱43,385,232.05,
respectively, and reconcile the balances of the PPE ledger accounts with the PPELC
as of year-end, and draw the necessary correcting entries for any error and/or
omission found.

We also recommended that the LCE require the: (i) City Accountant to
recognize/record the donated properties in the books of accounts in accordance with
Sections 27 and 28 of IPSAS 17; and City GSO Head to ensure that AREs/PARs are
prepared, issued to end-users, and regularly updated/renewed every three years
pursuant to Item 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024, and COA
Handbook on Property and Supply Management System.

Furthermore, we recommended that the City Accountant submit the Deed of
Donation for the 19 units of service vehicles acquired in CY 2023 as required.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the:

a. City Accountant to trace the unidentified beginning balances of the CIP
account, and verify the records or pertinent documents to establish their validity so
that necessary adjustments could be made;

b. City Accountant in coordination with the City Engineer to cause the transfer of the
above-cited completed project from the CIP account – Building and Other
Structures to the appropriate PPE account and Registry of Public Infrastructures; and

c. City Inventory and Disposal Committee to submit the RPCPPE for CIP
accounts as required by Section 124, Volume 1 of MNGAS for LGUs.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE) instruct the City Accountant, in coordination with the City
Treasurer, to strictly enforce the submission of financial reports, and documents
including Monthly BRS within the prescribed period/due dates as required in order
to ensure timely verification and audit of transactions, and to avoid suspensions or
disallowances in audit.

We also recommended that the LCE through the City Administrator require
the assigned representatives from each city office to immediately submit to the City
Accounting Office the required supporting documents found lacking before payment.

We reiterated our recommendations requiring the City Accountant, in
coordination with the City Treasurer/Budget Officer, and Project Coordinators in
charge of the fund transfers from NGAs, and LGUs to:
a. submit to the Audit Team copies of the Memoranda of Agreement (MOAs) or
equivalent documents relative to the fund transfers received from NGAs, and LGUs;
b. monitor submission to the concerned Source Agency (NGAS/LGUs) of
Liquidation Reports including the refund of any unused balance from the funds
received by the City;
c. strictly observe the time frame of scheduled implementation of the projects to
ensure efficient delivery of public services to the intended beneficiaries to avoid the
recurrence of similar lapses in the future; and
d. ensure submission of documents related to fund releases/transfers to the City,
along with the corresponding liquidation reports; and prepare and maintain a
separate subsidiary ledger/record as required.

We also recommended that the LCE, through the City Administrator, instruct
the City Accountant, City Treasurer/Budget Officer, and Project Coordinators In-
Charge to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December
13, 1994 on the releases/transfer of funds to the City Government.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) require the:

a. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman/Head Secretariat (Infra and
Goods), in coordination with the City Budget Officer, to submit the City’s Annual
Procurement Plan (APP) including Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP),
and strictly observe the regulations prescribed by the Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184
or Government Procurement Reform Act;

b. BAC Secretariat Heads (Infra and Goods) in coordination with General
Services Office (GSO) Head, to furnish the COA Audit Team with copies of all
Purchase Orders (POs) and Perfected Contracts including supporting documents and
the Notice of Delivery as required, and strictly observe the regulations prescribed by
COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 on Contracts/Purchase Orders;
and COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 on Notice of Delivery;

c. Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), to strictly comply with Section 37.4.1 of
the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 on the timely issuance of the Notice to Proceed
(NTP) to avoid delays in the project implementation and completion; and

d. City Engineer, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
and the City Accountant, to ensure strict compliance with Sections 62.2, 62.2.1 and
62.2.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 requiring the issuance of Certificate of
Acceptance one year after project completion or the Defects Liability Period and the
posting of surety (warranty) bond thereof, in order for the contractor to shoulder the
repair of any damaged portion of the project.

We also recommended that the City Accountant, in coordination with BAC
Secretariat Heads (Infra and Goods), ensure (i) the submission of the above-noted
requisite documents; (ii) that the required information and documents for
infrastructure projects and goods are complete before payment; (iii) the submission of
the DOLE-approved CSHP as required; and (iv) compliance with the provisions of
DOLE Department Order No. 13, Series of 1998 on CSHP.

We reiterated our recommendations that the LCE direct the CPDO, City
Engineer’s Office (CEO), and BAC refer to DILG, DBM Joint Memorandum Circular
(JMC) No. 01 in developing the City’s Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and PPAs for the
20% DF to improve the procurement and implementation of the City’s DF PPAs thereby,
attaining the desired socio-economic development outcomes and improvement in the
quality of life of the marginalized sector.

We also recommended that the LCE, in coordination with the LDC, CPDO
and City Budget Office, present/express the PPAs in detail charged under the 20%
DF in the AIP and Annual Budget as required under Section 317(b.3) of R.A. No.
7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. instruct GFPS to coordinate with the Local Finance Committee (LFC) in
formulating GAD PPAs that are achievable within the year, consider the appropriate
timing and regularly monitor their implementation/utilization so that necessary
interventions may be introduced at the earliest time and amend the GPB, if
warranted, to achieve its full implementation/utilization; and

b. require the LFC to come up with realistic income estimate that is attainable to
avoid shortfall in revenue collection and the eventual deferment of PPAs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) LDRRM
Officer in coordination with the CPD Officer to (i) include under a separate caption,
the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous
years in the LDRRMFIP; and (ii) continue utilizing the fund for the implementation
of projects and activities for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation
response, as well as rehabilitation and recovery; and (b) City Treasurer in
coordination with the City Accountant to facilitate the actual transfer of the
unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to a Special Trust Fund under the Trust
Liability DRRM account in accordance with the above-cited Circular.

We also recommended that the City Treasurer in coordination with the City
Accountant immediately liquidate the fund transfer from the Office of the President
and submit the liquidation report as required.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)
instruct the:

a. City Accountant, in coordination with the City Engineer and the BAC
Secretariat for Infrastructure, to: (i) submit the requisite documents noted, including
copies of the project logbooks as mandated; (ii) provide the As-Built plans for
completed projects, duly reviewed and certified by the City Engineer’s Office; (iii)
ensure that all required information and documentation for infrastructure projects
are complete prior to the release of payment; and (iv) adhere to the provisions of the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of R.A. No. 9184, as well as
Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 concerning the documentary requirements for
infrastructure projects.

b. HOPE, through the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), to strictly comply
with Section 37.4.1 of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 on the timely issuance of the Notice
to Proceed (NTP) to avoid delays in the project implementation and completion;

c. City Accountant in coordination with the BAC Secretariat-Infra to submit the
approved DOLE CSHP as required, and comply with the provisions of DOLE
Department Order No. 13, Series of 1998, on CSHP; and

d. City Engineer, in coordination with the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
and the City Accountant, to ensure strict compliance with Sections 62.2, 62.2.1 and
62.2.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 requiring the issuance of Certificate of
Acceptance one year after the project completion/Defects Liability Period, and the
posting of surety (warranty) bond thereof, thereby obligating the contractor to cover
the repair of any damaged portions of the project.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive, as Chairperson of the LSB,
agreed to require the:

LSB through the LSB Secretary in coordination with the City Accountant to –
a. ensure the submission of the SEF Annual Budget and SEF Utilization Reports
(Quarterly and Annually) to the concerned government agencies/offices; and monitor
the required timeline for the submission of SEF documents, and thereafter, the
posting of the SEF Utilization Reports to the appropriate platforms/channels in
accordance with Item 6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and

LSB through the LSB Secretary to –
b. ensure that the date received on the file or receiving copy of the SEF documents
is clearly indicated or affixed by the receiving government agencies/offices’
representative in order to determine compliance with the provision of the Joint
Circular on indicative timeline.

We reiterated our previous recommendations that the LCE, through the City
Administrator, instruct the CHRMO to:

a. setup policy guidelines to address the issues on the necessity and propriety of
hiring Job Order employees consistent/aligned with Section 77 of R.A. No. 7160 or
Local Government Code and Section 2, Rule IX of the Revised Omnibus Rules
Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292, and Section 1 (b) of Civil Service
Commission (CSC) Resolution No. 021480.

b. conduct review of the City’s existing Organizational Structure/Plantilla to
properly identify and establish the appropriate human resource complement, and
create additional permanent positions which may be considered for regular functions
of the agency; and select from among the City’s qualified Job Order employees those
who are qualified to fill the vacant permanent or casual positions:

c. reduce/limit the number of Job Order personnel based on the necessity of their
services, qualifications and performance; and conduct periodic performance
evaluations and assessments of Job Order personnel to avoid wastage of government
funds;

d. avoid the hiring of job order personnel whose functions overlap/duplicate
those of the City’s regular employees; and

e. coordinate with the City Accountant for the submission of copies of the JOs'
approved contracts to the COA Audit Team, as required, to verify the necessity and
veracity of their services.

We also recommended that Management integrate the screening process in the
hiring of job order personnel and create a committee that will evaluate the JOs’
educational qualifications vis-à-vis salary rate, and job assignment.

Furthermore, we recommended that the City Budget Officer in coordination
with the City Treasurer revisit the budget allocation for Job Order personnel
considering the huge number of job orders being hired.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct the CPDO, CEO and BAC to:
a) require end-user/offices or implementing units to formulate their PPMPs respective Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) for their different programs, activities, and projects (PAPs) for consolidation into the required APP;
b) Consider the appropriate timing and phasing of the implementation of PPAs and set out clearly defined roles and responsibilities in project planning, implementation and monitoring so that the necessary and timely intervention can be made to ensure on-time completion of PPAs; and
c) refer to DILG, DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 01 in developing the City’s Annual Investment Plan and PPAs for the 20% DF to improve the procurement and implementation of the City’s 20% DF PPAs thereby, attaining the desired socio-economic development outcomes and improvement in the quality of life of the marginalized sector.
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the City Budget Officer to maintain a Registry of Appropriations, Allotment and Obligations for Continuing Appropriations as required in Section 2.2 of COA Circular No. 2006-002.
observations.managementAction:
All offices submitted FY 2022 and 2023 Proposed Organizational Structure/Plantilla. The City Administrator has a focal person and formulated a monitoring scheme to monitor the timely implementation of PPAs and instructed to explicitly state and document the role and responsibilities in all project phases.

The Annual Investment Plan is aligned with the DILG-DBM Joint MC No. 01.

The City Budget Office is maintaining Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for Continuing Appropriations.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.; The City Implemented a monitoring scheme and instructed the roles and responsibilities to be documented.; The City Budget Office is maintaining Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for Continuing Appropriations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. direct the LDRRMO together with the LDRRMC, to prepare and submit annually the LDRRMFIP for the DRRM program of the LGU subject to further amendments, if the need arises, in compliance with the prescribed format with details such as allotment class, including list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, among others, as prescribed by the above quoted regulations;
b. ensure the utilization of the funds for the specific purpose it was created; and
c. instruct the City Accountant to (i) fully disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended LDRMMF; and (ii) prepare and submit the monthly report on the sources and utilization of DRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMO has prepared the LDRRMFIP in prescribed format and submitted the same. The LCE instructed the City Accountant to ensure to fully disclose in the Notes to FS for CY 2023. The City Accountant together with LDRRMO prepared and submitted monthly reports on the sources and utilization of DRRMF.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.; (ii) ensure to fully disclose in the Notes to FS for CY 2023.; (ii) prepare and submit the monthly report on the sources and utilization of DRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years' recommendations that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the CENRO:
a. intensify and sustain the current information drive and awareness campaign on ecological solid waste management, particularly at the household and business level, in coordination with the Barangay officials, following the strategies identified under the approved 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU;
b. exercise strong political will to implement the applicable ordinances pertaining to solid waste management; and
c. request the Barangays to adopt the City ordinances pertaining to the solid waste management.

Additionally, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive allot a budget or provide the required funding for the rehabilitation of the MRF and other infrastructure and technical support in order to fully comply with the requirements of RA 9003 and achieve the eventual volume reduction of waste.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has imposed solid waste management ordinances in which Barangays were mandated and instructed to adopt the same. The LCE noted the recommendation. Additionally, the LGU has started programs to rehabilitate MRF and provide technical support and appropriate budget allocation.
observations.reason: The LGU has imposed solid waste management ordinances in which Barangays were mandated and instructed to adopt the same.; The LCE noted the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year's recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the City Accountant, in coordination with the City Treasurer, to strictly enforce the submission of financial documents including reports within the prescribed period/due dates as required in order not to delay the verification and audit of transactions.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office and Treasury Office have agreed with the recommendation. The City Accounting Office is continually improving its systems and processes to streamline the document flow as the sheer volume of the documents pose quite a challenge to current manpower and limited storage. The CAO manually processes approximately 20,000 disbursement and journal vouchers, with the preparation of the physical voucher, and the recording of the same, as separate activities. This year, they also experienced 30 percent attrition in their accounting workforce and attributed the resignation of former employees to the growing BPO job market in Clark. Nonetheless, they have a plan and budget in place to automate and integrate their financial systems to improve timeliness of submission even with their limited manpower.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant (a) maintain subsidiary ledgers of real property taxpayers based on the certified list of real property taxpayers submitted by the City Treasurer and post the payments of taxpayers as they occur to reflect their updated balances; (b) regularly reconcile the balance of RPT/SET Receivable account with the list of taxpayers with paid/unpaid real property tax dues maintained by the City Treasurer and the draw journal entry vouchers to adjust the balances thereof; and (c) avail of information technology, such as database or spreadsheet applications, to facilitate subsidiary ledger-keeping.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) reconstitute the Inventory Committee, supported by competent staff, and direct them to conduct and complete the physical count of PPE accounts every year and render a report thereon within the prescribed period, copy furnished the GSO and the Accounting Division; (b) require the General Services Officer to (i) prepare and submit the complete Report on the Physical Count of PPE duly reconciled with the accounting and property records; and (ii) maintain Property Cards for all PPE accounts per category; and (c) direct the City Accountant to reconcile the PPELCs with the PPE General Ledger accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) City Accountant to prepare adjusting entries reclassifying the ₱13.5 million funds transferred to the barangays from Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account to Due from Local Government Units (LGUs) account then, upon  receipt of liquidation from barangays, debit the appropriate asset or expense accounts and credit the Due from LGUs account; and (b) Punong Barangays to submit liquidation documents to the City Accounting Office pursuant to the Memorandum of Agreement and to serve as mechanism, aside from physical inspection, in the monitoring of the utilization of the fund transfers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) take earnest efforts to augment the manpower complement of the Accounting Office with competent staff so that accurate and timely financial reporting is not disrupted; and (b)  instruct a). the staff of the City Accountant  to secure authorization from the City Treasurer to receive copies of bank statement from the banks to pave the way for the preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements; and b). the City Accountant or his staff to (i) initiate the conduct of reconciliation of the Cash in Bank account with the depository bank within ten (10) days from receipt of the Bank Statements and (ii) submit the same to the auditor within the prescribed period in consonance with Item 3.2. of COA Circular 96-001; and (iii) perform a monthly reconciliation of the City Treasury collection accounts with the general/subsidiary ledger balance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) refrain from extending financial assistance to MAGEA and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 especially in providing financial assistance only to non-governmental organizations (NGOs) and/or people’s organizations (POs); (b) advise the MAGEA officers and the City’s Legal Officer to revisit/revise the Collective Negotiation Agreement (CNA) with Mabalacat Government Employees Association (MAGEA) to harmonize all provisions with the Amended Rules and Regulations of Executive Order No. 180 dated June 1, 1987; (c) submit the revised CNA to the Civil Service Commission – Personnel Relations Officer for registration and review to determine the non-negotiable or unenforceable items in the said agreement before enforcing it; (d) refrain from extending additional benefits to employees with no firm basis even if funded/disbursed through other means like financial assistance to MAGEA; and (e) require the MAGEA to refund the ₱1,125,000.00 illegally disbursed in CY 2016 and ₱373,000.00 in February 2017.

We recommended that the (a) Chief Local Executive and the Sanggunian Panlungsod refrain from extending bonus/incentives to the City employees with no firm legal basis to prevent audit disallowances from illegal disbursement of public funds; and (b) Local Chief Executive and Accountant require the refund from the recipients and approving officers of illegally disbursed funds totaling ₱22,018,500.00.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the City Budget Officer to (i) include all the salaries of Mabalacat City College and Birthing Stations employees in the Personal Services of General Fund on the next budget preparation because both entities have not satisfied the income generating requirement of an economic enterprise pursuant to DBM’s Budget Operations Manual (BOM) for Local Government Units (An Updated Version of the June 2005 Edition); (ii) submit explanations and legal basis for the excess of Actual PS over the 45% PS Limitation for the years 2010 to 2016 as well as the excess of the PS Budget for the years 2010 to 2013 for being contrary to Section 325 (a) of the Local Government Code and Section 4(1) of Presidential Decree No. 1445; and (iii) properly review Obligation Requests to ensure that expenses especially salaries are charged to the respective offices  in order to have an accurate Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations in accordance with item 2.2 of COA Circular 2006-002; and (b) reduce the number of employees, particularly those employed under Job Order personnel whose services are redundant and/or not really necessary to comply with the 45% Personal Services Limitation pursuant to Section 325 (a) of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that the City Accountant (a) in coordination with other offices involve, work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies; and (b) henceforth, ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before they are processed and paid.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) cause the submission of the pertinent documents to support the grant of cash advance for confidential expenses; and (b) henceforth, ensure that officials concerned strictly follow the provisions of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 on the grant of cash advance for confidential purposes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Accountant to (a) ensure that claims are supported with all the necessary documents and properly signed by authorized officials before any payment is made; and (b) submit the required documents stated above for the evaluation of the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) request the Sanggunian Panlungsod to revisit the Mabalacat City Passenger Terminal Code of 2013 and request the inclusion of  protective provisions to address the issues on internal control, audit and examination of the books of the JV and the reportorial responsibilities of  the Joint Venture Board (JVB) in supporting the computations on the City’s share from the JV; (b) require the Management Team of the JV or JVB to submit monthly reports duly certified/signed by the proper signatories showing the detailed computations of share from JV remitted to the City Treasurer; (c) instruct the supervisor of the City employees assigned at the MCPTC to submit to the City Accountant the copies of duly certified daily lists of buses entering the said terminal complex, including information on the first and last control numbers/series per booklet of terminal tickets/receipts issued by the cashiers within the day; (d) instruct the City Accountant to assign an Accounting Staff to monitor the submission of the duly certified daily lists of buses entering the terminal complex and to review the completeness and accuracy thereof; (e) require the Management Team of the JV/JVB to (i) submit monthly statement of accountability for accountable forms which shows the control number/series of terminal tickets/receipts purchased and issued within the month and balance of unused tickets/receipts at the end of the month; and (ii) to support with reasonable documentations the monthly check/cash disbursements for authorized expenditures to establish their validity and propriety; and (f) require the JVB to (i) register the JV with the Securities and Exchange Commission pursuant to the NEDA guidelines on JV with private entities dated May 3, 2013; and (ii) issue BIR-compliant tickets/receipts to the public utility buses using the terminal complex and passengers using the comfort rooms pursuant to Section 3 of BIR Revenue Regulations no. 18-2012 dated October 22, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City HRMO and HRMO III of MCC to submit written explanations on the payment of full salary of 27 casual employees despite rendering less than 40 working hours in a week or 80 hours for the period December 1-15, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) address with urgency the manpower issues faced by the City Accounting Office; (b) require the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to (i) strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the verification and audit of transactions and the recording of transactions in the appropriate period; and (ii) observe the due dates in the submission to the Audit Team of the required financial reports and documents;  (c) enjoin all officers and offices concerned to comply with the submission of adequate documents before forwarding any claim to the Accounting Department for processing.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the CSWMO (a) involve closely the Barangay officials and civic leaders in the strict implementation on the existing environmental laws and intensify the information campaign at the household and business level on the ordinances issued by the City; (b) seek the help of private companies/organizations in the conduct of a more aggressive and intensive information campaign on the proper segregation of waste; (c) formulate solid waste management programs that would encourage and motivate the public to give their cooperation and full support; and (d) strictly implement the Environment Code especially its fines and penalties provision for violators.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the City Budget Officer, the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to coordinate with one another to (a) establish the  amount of unutilized balance of the LDDRMF based on the realized income from regular sources and compute for the unutilized balance backed up by cash for transfer to the special trust fund; and (b) effect the transfer of the amount established under the first procedure from the general fund to a special trust fund; and (c) draw the journal entry voucher to recognize the said transfer of the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF to a special trust fund.
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) to formulate a Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan every year to serve as guide in the utilization of LDRRMF and submit it to the LCE for approval.

We recommended that Members of the City Local School Board (a) request for inputs from the Deped official/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations such as Department of Education (DepEd), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Joint Circular No. 1, s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998 (amended by DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017) prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; (b) submit the details of SEF Budget immediately upon approval to the City’s Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) for bidding of supplies, equipment, and infrastructures; and (c) maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the PPAs, and take appropriate actions such as realignment or reprogramming of budget, if necessary.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the GAD Focal Person to evaluate the causes of the non-implementation of the five GAD PPAs to gain lessons or inputs thereon; and (b) require the City Planning and Development Officer (CPDO) to hasten the completion of Community Based Management System for the establishment of the GAD database in time for the next GAD Plan and Budget preparation.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive, through the City Accountant, (a) require all the persons deemed liable on the disallowance to immediately refund the full amount of disallowance; and (b) direct all persons responsible to submit the required documents to settle the notices of suspension.

(b) henceforth, ensure that officials concerned strictly follow the provisions of COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 on the grant of cash advance for confidential purposes.
